title: Operator Remapping
subtitle: Schankian Primitives as Place Cells on the Meaning Manifold

children.children: Hippocampal place cells remap when context changes. What if Schankian primitives do the same?

children: This tutorial explores a cross-disciplinary connection: the same neuron fires at different locations depending on context, and the same cognitive primitive (ATRANS, PTRANS) may operate with fundamentally different geometry depending on where you are on the meaning manifold. Same label, different algebra.

children.children: In 1971, John O'Keefe discovered neurons in the hippocampus that fire when an animal is at a specific location in its environment. These 'place cells' collectively form a cognitive map — an internal representation of space.

children.definition: A hippocampal neuron that fires selectively when the animal occupies a specific region (its 'place field') in a given environment.
The ensemble of place cell activations that collectively encode the animal's position. First proposed by Tolman (1948), neurally instantiated by O'Keefe & Dostrovsky (1971).
When the same place cell changes its firing pattern — firing at a different location, at a different rate, or not at all — in response to a context change.

children.children: The key discovery: place cells don't encode fixed locations. They REMAP when context changes. The same cell that fires at the north wall of a square room might fire at the center of a circular room, or go silent entirely. The cell's identity persists, but its meaning is context-dependent.
Neuroscientists distinguish two forms of remapping, and both have direct analogs in our theory of semantic primitives:

children.definition: The place field stays in the same location, but the cell's firing rate changes. Same spatial meaning, different intensity. Analog: the same ATRANS operates in the same structural role, but with different conviction or urgency.

The entire map reorganizes — place fields shift to completely different locations. The cell's spatial meaning changes entirely. Analog: ATRANS in a legal context has a completely different argument structure, temporal profile, and inference chain than ATRANS on a playground.

props.from: Place cells in a rat's hippocampus
props.to: Schankian primitives in a language processor

children.children: A place cell fires at the north wall in Room A and the center in Room B — same neuron, different meaning. ATRANS means 'toss a ball' on a playground and 'transfer fiduciary responsibility' in a courtroom — same primitive, different operator algebra. In both cases, identity is preserved but content remaps with context.
Let's lay out the full correspondence between hippocampal remapping and operator remapping:
Place cells fire at specific locations → Primitives fire for specific event types
Remapping when context changes → Operator algebra changes when register/world changes
Rate remapping (same field, different rate) → Conviction modulation (same primitive, different confidence)
Global remapping (completely different map) → Cross-modal shift (ATRANS in fictional vs actual world is structurally different)
Cognitive map consolidation during sleep → Operator discovery during wake-sleep compression

Polysemy says a WORD has multiple meanings. Remapping says an OPERATOR has multiple geometries. The difference matters: polysemy is about surface forms ('bank' = river bank or financial bank), while remapping is about the primitive itself. ATRANS doesn't have multiple meanings — it always means 'transfer of control.' But what 'transfer' and 'control' actually entail (argument structure, temporal profile, legal implications, reversibility) depends on where you are on the manifold. The label is topologically invariant; the content is geometrically variable.
Let's make this concrete with a single primitive — ATRANS (abstract transfer) — and see how it remaps across four manifold locations:

props.title: ATRANS Remapping Explorer
props.description: See how the same Schankian primitive changes its properties across different manifold locations (registers/contexts).

content.playground:
**ATRANS on a Playground**

• Surface verb: 'give', 'toss', 'share'
• Arguments: (child₁, child₂, toy)
• Temporal profile: Instantaneous
• Reversibility: Easy — 'give it back'
• Legal weight: None
• Inferences: toy changes hands, social bond reinforced
• Composition: mail = ATRANS ∘ PTRANS (hand it over physically)
• Failed ATRANS: 'He won't share' — social consequence only
content.legal:
**ATRANS in Legal Context**

• Surface verb: 'transfer', 'convey', 'assign', 'bequeath'
• Arguments: (transferor, transferee, asset, consideration, instrument)
• Temporal profile: Extended — effective upon execution of instrument
• Reversibility: Difficult — requires counter-instrument or court order
• Legal weight: Maximal — fiduciary obligations, chain of custody
• Inferences: ownership changes, tax implications, regulatory notification
• Composition: sale = ATRANS(asset) ∘ ATRANS⁻¹(consideration) — bilateral
• Failed ATRANS: Breach of contract — legal consequence
content.digital:
**ATRANS in Digital Context**

• Surface verb: 'send', 'share', 'forward', 'upload'
• Arguments: (sender, recipient, data, channel)
• Temporal profile: Near-instantaneous but asynchronous
• Reversibility: Impossible — 'unsend' is cosmetic, recipient may have copied
• Legal weight: Variable — depends on content (DMCA, GDPR)
• Inferences: data duplicated (not moved!), no physical PTRANS involved
• Composition: email = ATRANS with NO PTRANS (Schank's decomposition breaks!)
• Failed ATRANS: 'Message not delivered' — retry or escalate
content.fictional:
**ATRANS in Fictional World**

• Surface verb: 'bestow', 'grant', 'bequeath' (a magic ring)
• Arguments: (giver, receiver, magical_artifact, prophecy_constraint)
• Temporal profile: Mythic — may span generations
• Reversibility: Governed by narrative logic, not physics or law
• Legal weight: None in actual world; absolute in story-world
• Inferences: quarantined to FICTIONAL modality — cannot pollute ACTUAL
• Composition: quest = PTRANS ∘ trials ∘ ATRANS(artifact) — narrative arc
• Failed ATRANS: Plot twist — the artifact rejects the unworthy

children.children: Notice: in the digital context, Schank's original decomposition 'mail = ATRANS ∘ PTRANS' breaks down — email involves no physical transfer at all, and the 'transfer' is actually a duplication. The primitive has remapped so thoroughly that its composition rules change.
The Hydra architecture (a multi-head linguistic preprocessing pipeline) makes operator remapping explicit and mechanical:
Layer 0: Register/World Head → determines manifold coordinate z (per-token)
Layer 1: Manifold-Aware Embeddings → embed(token, z) from sparse landmarks
Layer 2: Parsing Heads → operate with REMAPPED operators at location z
Layer 3: Body (Mamba SSM) → processes fully grounded, correctly-remapped tokens

The register head is the hippocampal context signal. When it outputs z = 'legal', all downstream operators remap to their legal-context geometry. The thematic role head then tags 'transferred' with the correct argument structure for legal ATRANS (5 arguments, not 3), and the body generates text that respects legal inference patterns.

props.title: Why does remapping have to be Layer 0?

children.children: Because operator geometry is context-dependent, and all subsequent processing depends on having the right geometry. If the phrase structure head parses 'She gave him the cold shoulder' without knowing we're in colloquial register, it will try to find a physical object for 'the cold shoulder.' If the thematic head assigns roles without knowing we're in legal context, it won't allocate slots for 'consideration' and 'instrument.' Register must come first because it tells every other head which rulebook to use — just as hippocampal context signals must arrive before place cells can fire correctly.
If ATRANS changes its argument structure, temporal profile, and inference chain across manifold locations, what makes it still ATRANS? The answer: topological role.
The Topological Invariant:

children: ATRANS always mediates transfer-of-control between agents. In every context, there is a giver, a receiver, and something whose control changes hands. The geometric details (how many arguments, how long it takes, whether it's reversible, what inferences follow) vary with manifold location. But the topological role — 'this is a transfer event' — is invariant.

props.from: A coffee cup and a donut
props.to: ATRANS in playground and ATRANS in courtroom

children.children: A coffee cup and a donut are topologically equivalent (both have one hole) even though they look completely different geometrically. Similarly, 'tossing a ball' and 'transferring custody' are topologically equivalent as ATRANS events (both have a giver, receiver, and transferred-thing) even though their geometric properties (argument count, temporal profile, legal weight) are completely different.
This isn't just a metaphor — it generates specific, testable predictions:

props.title: Predictions of Operator Remapping
props.description: Click each prediction to see the experimental design and expected outcome.

content.discovery:
**Prediction 1: Register-stratified operator discovery finds different primitive libraries.**

Experiment: Run wake-sleep operator discovery separately on legal text, casual conversation, scientific prose, and fiction. Compare the discovered primitive libraries.

Expected: The topological structure is shared (all domains have transfer, movement, communication operators) but the geometric details differ (legal ATRANS has 5 argument slots; playground ATRANS has 3). The MDL-optimal compression uses the same number of primitives but with different internal structure.
content.transfer:
**Prediction 2: Cross-register transfer is partial.**

Experiment: Train an operator discovery system on legal text. Test on casual conversation without retraining.

Expected: The system recognizes ATRANS events (topological match) but makes systematic errors on argument structure (geometric mismatch). Legal-trained ATRANS expects 'consideration' and 'instrument' slots that don't exist in casual context. Transfer learning succeeds at the category level but fails at the slot level.
content.composition:
**Prediction 3: Operator composition is manifold-local.**

Experiment: Test whether 'mail = ATRANS ∘ PTRANS' holds across registers.

Expected: In physical mail context, yes. In email context, the PTRANS component vanishes entirely — digital ATRANS is standalone. In legal context, 'service of process = ATRANS ∘ PTRANS' but with strict ordering constraints (physical delivery required). The same compositional formula produces different results at different manifold locations.
content.blank_head:
**Prediction 4: Hydra's blank head discovers remapping boundaries.**

Experiment: Train Hydra with its fourth (discovery) head on mixed-register text.

Expected: The blank head learns to fire at tokens where the manifold coordinate shifts abruptly — code-switching points, register shifts, genre transitions. These are the points where operators need to be re-selected, analogous to place cells resetting at environment boundaries.

children.children: Srivastava et al. (2025) independently found 'algorithmic primitives' in LLM reasoning by clustering activation patterns. Their key finding: the primitives are TASK-SPECIFIC. The algorithmic primitives for solving TSP are different from those for 3SAT or graph navigation.

This is exactly manifold-local operators! The reasoning manifold has different local primitives depending on the task domain, just as ATRANS has different local geometry depending on the social domain. Their work provides empirical evidence from mechanistic interpretability that operators remap with context.

props.title: Why this resolves Schank's universalism debate

children.children: Schank was BOTH right and wrong. Right that there's a finite vocabulary of primitive operations — you always need something that plays the ATRANS role, something that plays the PTRANS role. Wrong that the vocabulary is fixed in its content. The vocabulary is TOPOLOGICALLY fixed (there's always a transfer operator) but GEOMETRICALLY variable (what transfer actually does depends on context). This reconciles Schank's universalism with the obvious context-sensitivity of natural language, and it's the same resolution that topology gives to the 'coffee cup = donut' puzzle: identity at the level of structure, diversity at the level of shape.

children.question: What is 'global remapping' in hippocampal place cells?
options: The cell fires more frequently at the same location
The cell's place field shifts to a completely different location when context changes
New place cells are created for each new environment
children.explanation: Global remapping means the entire cognitive map reorganizes — the same cell now fires at a different location. The cell persists, but its spatial meaning changes entirely. This is analogous to ATRANS changing its argument structure when you move from playground to courtroom.

children.question: Why does Schank's decomposition 'mail = ATRANS ∘ PTRANS' break down for email?
options: Email doesn't involve any transfer at all
Email involves physical transfer we can't see
Digital ATRANS involves duplication, not physical movement — the PTRANS component vanishes
ATRANS only applies to physical objects
children.explanation: When you email someone a document, the data is COPIED, not physically moved. There is no PTRANS (physical transfer) component. The operator has remapped so thoroughly at the 'digital' manifold location that its composition rules change.

children.question: What is the 'topological invariant' of a Schankian primitive under remapping?
options: The number of arguments stays the same
The surface verb stays the same
The structural role (e.g., 'mediates transfer-of-control') is preserved even when geometric details change
The inference chain stays the same
children.explanation: Like a coffee cup and a donut being topologically equivalent (one hole each) despite looking completely different, ATRANS on a playground and ATRANS in a courtroom both mediate transfer-of-control — the topological role is invariant. But the geometric details (argument count, temporal profile, legal implications) change with manifold location.

children.question: In the Hydra architecture, why must register detection happen at Layer 0 (before parsing heads)?
options: Register is the easiest thing to detect so it should go first
It's faster to detect register before syntax
All other heads need to know which operator algebra applies before they can assign roles or parse structure
children.explanation: If you don't know the register, you don't know which operators are in play, which argument structures to expect, or which parsing rules to apply. 'She gave him the cold shoulder' needs colloquial register to avoid searching for a physical object. Register is the context signal that triggers the correct remapping for all downstream processing.

children.children: Key references that informed this brainstorm:

children.definition: Learning Place Cells and Remapping by Decoding the Cognitive Map. eLife. RNNs trained on navigation learn place cells that spontaneously remap with context — the structural parallel to operator remapping.

Algorithmic Primitives and Compositional Geometry of Reasoning in Language Models. arXiv:2510.15987. Task-specific algorithmic primitives discovered via activation clustering — empirical evidence for manifold-local operators.

Geometric Transformation of Cognitive Maps for Generalization across Hippocampal-Prefrontal Circuits. Cell Reports. How the brain geometrically transforms cognitive maps for cross-context generalization — analogous to cross-register primitive transfer.

Robust Speech Perception: Recognize the Familiar, Generalize to the Similar, and Adapt to the Novel. Psychological Review. The ideal adapter framework — listeners maintain multiple generative models and rapidly select the right one, the closest existing framework to Hydra's Layer 0.

Conceptual Information Processing. The original Conceptual Dependency theory proposing universal primitive actions — the claim this brainstorm both validates (topologically) and refines (geometrically).
